content.problemStatement:
Today Chef wants to evaluate the dishes of his NNN students. He asks each one to cook a dish and present it to him.

Chef loves his secret ingredient, and only likes dishes with at least XXX grams of it.
Given NNN, XXX and the amount of secret ingredient used by each student AiA_iAi​, find out whether Chef will like at least one dish.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line of each testcase contains two integers NNN
(number of students) and XXX (minimum amount of secret ingredient that a dish must contain for Chef to like it).
The next line contains NNN space separated integers, AiA_iAi​ denoting the amount of secret ingredient used by the students in their dishes.
###Output:
For each testcase, print a single string "YES" if Chef likes at least one dish. Otherwise, print "NO". (Without quotes).

###Constraints:


1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
1≤N≤10001 \leq N \leq 10001≤N≤1000
1≤X≤10000001 \leq X \leq 10000001≤X≤1000000
1≤Ai≤10000001 \leq A_i \leq 10000001≤Ai​≤1000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Then, for each test case, the first line contains two integers N and X, where N is the number of students and X is the minimum amount of secret ingredient required. The next line contains N space-separated integers, each representing the amount of secret ingredient used by a student.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'YES' if at least one dish contains the minimum required amount of secret ingredient, and 'NO' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, no student uses at least 100 grams of secret ingredient. In the second test case, the student who uses 50 grams of secret ingredient meets the requirement. In the third test case, the student who uses 60 grams of secret ingredient meets the requirement.

solution.approach: For each test case, iterate through the amounts of secret ingredient used by each student. If any amount is greater than or equal to the minimum required amount, print 'YES' and move to the next test case. If no such amount is found after checking all students, print 'NO'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum number of students in a test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — as we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and output.
